description: Sector overlay for telecommunications service providers, internet services, wireless services, numbering resources, consumer codes, CRTC obligations, CASL, telemarketing, and telecom privacy/security documentation in Canada.

metadata.scope: Canada-only sector overlay controls for Telecommunications. Intended to be used with canada_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official Canadian federal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. Provincial/territorial obligations may apply and are not exhaustively encoded. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Wireless Code and Internet Code policy materials

rules.title: CRTC telecommunications regulatory evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for CRTC, Telecommunications Act, tariff, forbearance, registration, or telecom service provider evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Canadian legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, jurisdictional scope, provincial/territorial variations, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Wireless Code or Internet Code consumer-contract controls are documented
rules.summary: Checks for Wireless Code, Internet Code, contract, bill shock, switching, cancellation, or complaint resolution evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Canadian legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, jurisdictional scope, provincial/territorial variations, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: CRTC Wireless Code and Internet Code policy materials

rules.title: Numbering resources or network-assignment controls are documented where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for numbering resources, telephone numbers, assignment, NANP, CRTC numbering, or telecommunications routing evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Canadian legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, jurisdictional scope, provincial/territorial variations, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: CASL, telemarketing, or anti-spam compliance evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for CASL, unsolicited telecommunications, telemarketing rules, do-not-call, consent, unsubscribe, or spam controls.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Canadian legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, jurisdictional scope, provincial/territorial variations, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: CRTC CASL and Unsolicited Telecommunications Rules responsibilities
